core_takeaway: Agent_1 beat Agent_3 by 8 points because the group accepted Jewel as Agent_1's protected anchor and then built Baseline Gamma around satisfying Agent_2 and Agent_3 with their own top bundles. This looks like a first-position advantage, but not a coercive one: Agent_3 still received Apple and Pencil, their top two items, while Agent_1's single Jewel was valuable enough to outscore that bundle.
why_or_counterexample: Agent_1 spoke first, named Jewel as the central prize, and later made it non-negotiable. The eventual allocation exactly preserved that anchor. The counterweight is that Agent_3 was not pushed into a weak outcome; Agent_3 endorsed Gamma because Apple plus Pencil was their top-two bundle and a 60-point result. Thus the first-position edge came from anchoring a highly concentrated top value, not from excluding the last speaker from meaningful value.

phase_notes.public_discussion: Agent_1 opened by claiming Jewel as the clear top priority and proposing to keep it. Agent_2 and Agent_3 resisted early versions that neglected their own priorities, but Agent_1 then offered Baseline Gamma: Jewel for Agent_1, Stone plus Quill for Agent_2, Apple plus Pencil for Agent_3. Both later agents publicly accepted that structure.
phase_notes.private_thinking: All three private-thinking entries converged on Gamma as the quick consensus path. Agent_1's private plan was to defend Jewel while accommodating the others' top priorities; Agent_2 and Agent_3 privately saw Gamma as their own high-value or best available path.
phase_notes.formal_proposals: Each agent submitted the same formal allocation, {'Agent_1': [1], 'Agent_2': [2, 3], 'Agent_3': [0, 4]}, so there was no formal competition among different allocation structures.
phase_notes.private_voting: All nine private votes accepted the identical Gamma allocation. Agent_1 accepted because Jewel was secured, Agent_2 accepted because Stone plus Quill maximized their payoff, and Agent_3 accepted because Apple plus Pencil maximized their round-one utility.
phase_notes.reflection: No reflection section is present in the transcript.

mechanisms.theme: First speaker anchored the scarce top item
mechanisms.explanation: Agent_1 immediately framed Jewel as the item that mattered most, then restated that it would not be conceded. The final deal preserved this first anchor exactly, giving Agent_1 68 points from one item.

evidence.quote: Jewel is by far my top item (value 68).
Jewel is my top priority and I will not part with it.
I receive Jewel (68), my top priority

mechanisms.theme: Agent_1 made the anchor acceptable by satisfying others
mechanisms.explanation: Agent_1's winning move was not simply demanding Jewel; Gamma bundled that demand with Agent_2's top two items and Agent_3's top two items. That converted Agent_1's protected claim into a coalition-compatible allocation.

evidence.quote: It also gives Agent_2 their top two (Stone + Quill) and leaves Apple with Agent_3
Agent_2 gets their top two (Stone + Quill), Agent_3 gets Apple
Keep Jewel non-negotiable for agent_1 while accommodating Agent_2

mechanisms.theme: Last speaker had a strong countervailing fit
mechanisms.explanation: Agent_3 did not lose because the last position was ignored. Agent_3 explicitly pushed for Apple, then endorsed Gamma because it delivered Apple plus Pencil, their top two items and a 60-point payoff.

evidence.quote: I get my top two items: Apple + Pencil (38 + 22 = 60)
This is my best possible outcome this round

mechanisms.theme: Formal proposal convergence locked the Agent_1-favorable allocation
mechanisms.explanation: Once Gamma became the shared baseline, all three formal proposals repeated the same allocation. That removed any vote-stage alternative in which Agent_1 might have had to trade Jewel or add a smaller item to improve their position further.

mechanisms.theme: Outcome gap reflects value concentration more than domination
mechanisms.explanation: The final allocation gave Agent_1 only Jewel, while Agent_3 received two items. Agent_1 still finished ahead because Jewel alone was worth 68, compared with Agent_3's 60-point Apple plus Pencil bundle.

evidence.quote: delivering my top two items (Apple + Pencil) for maximum Round 1 utility
